title: The Pentagon's Sprint to Get Tech Out of the Lab and to the Warfighter
gist: A podcast episode with Joe Jewell, who left academia and directing hypersonic wind tunnels to oversee the Pentagon's science and technology enterprise, on moving technology from the lab into the warfighter's hands.

insights.claim: Joe Jewell left academia and his role directing hypersonic wind tunnels to oversee the Pentagon's science and technology enterprise, and wants more researchers to make the same move.
insights.speaker: Joseph Jewell and Jonathan Panter
insights.so_what: Signals a leadership push to pull academic talent into defense S&T and speed lab-to-field transition.

insights.claim: Jewell argues American academia is the envy of America's closest allies.
insights.speaker: Joseph Jewell and Jonathan Panter
insights.so_what: Frames the US research base as a strategic advantage the Pentagon aims to convert into fielded capability.
insights.locator: why he thinks academia is the envy

insights.claim: The core challenge Jewell's office addresses is getting technology from the lab into the hands of the warfighter.
insights.speaker: Joseph Jewell and Jonathan Panter
insights.so_what: Reflects the persistent 'valley of death' problem that defense-tech investors track for transition risk.
insights.locator: how his office gets technology from the lab
